seoTitle: Add Branching Logic to a Microsoft Forms Survey
metaDescription: Route respondents to different questions based on their answers in Microsoft Forms using conditional branching, without messy manual filtering.
intro: Showing every question to every respondent leads to irrelevant prompts and higher drop-off rates. Microsoft Forms' branching logic lets you route people to specific follow-up questions or sections based on what they already answered, keeping the experience relevant and your data clean.

steps.heading: Open the question you want to branch from
steps.body: Select the multi-choice question where you want different answers to lead down different paths.

steps.heading: Open the branching menu
steps.body: Click the three-dot More Settings icon on the question and choose Add Branching to open the routing panel.

steps.heading: Set a destination for each answer
steps.body: Use the Go To dropdown next to each answer option to choose which question or section it should jump to.

steps.heading: Check the default path
steps.body: Review the default navigation for any questions that don't have explicit branching rules so respondents aren't dropped into a dead end.

steps.heading: End short paths cleanly
steps.body: For answers that should skip the rest of the form, set the Go To destination to End of the Form so those respondents submit immediately.

faq.question: Can I test branching before sending the form out?
faq.answer: Yes, use the Preview button to click through your form and confirm each answer routes to the right destination.

faq.question: What happens if I don't set a branching rule for an answer?
faq.answer: Unbranched answers follow the form's default sequential order, so it's worth reviewing every option.

faq.question: Can branching send someone backward to an earlier question?
faq.answer: Forms is designed for forward-only routing, so structure your questions to avoid circular logic loops.

faq.question: Does branching work with quizzes as well as regular forms?
faq.answer: Yes, the same branching setup is available on both standard forms and quizzes.
